ConfigFile.description:
The on-disk Config_File loaded via the ``--config`` CLI flag.

A ``ConfigFile`` carries process-wide settings (Ollama base URL, where
to discover suites, where to write reports, logging verbosity, and
dataset-sourcing defaults) plus an embedded :class:`RunConfig`
describing the Run to execute.

``hf_cache_dir`` defaults to ``None`` so the Backend can fall back to
the HuggingFace ``datasets`` library's own cache resolution (``$HF_HOME``
or ``~/.cache/huggingface``) without the Config_File having to encode
platform-specific paths.

dataset_mode.description: Default ``dataset_mode`` for adapter-backed Evaluation_Suites (Requirement 17.3). Individual suite files may override this per-suite. ``local`` means read from disk; ``remote`` means stream from the HuggingFace Hub at Run time.
dataset_mode.title: Dataset Mode

hf_cache_dir.description: Directory used by the HuggingFace ``datasets`` library for its on-disk cache. When ``None`` the library's own default is used (``$HF_HOME`` or ``~/.cache/huggingface``).
hf_cache_dir.title: Hf Cache Dir

log_level.description: Global log verbosity (Requirement 10.6). The CLI ``--log-level`` flag overrides this value at runtime.
log_level.title: Log Level

ollama_base_url.description: Base URL of the Ollama_Server (Requirement 1.1). Must be an absolute ``http://`` or ``https://`` URL.
ollama_base_url.title: Ollama Base Url

output_dir.description: Directory where Run_Reports (``runs/{run_id}/report.json`` and ``report.md``) are written (Requirement 8.1).
output_dir.title: Output Dir

suites_dir.description: Directory containing Evaluation_Suite files to discover at Run start (Requirement 3.1). Required; there is no sensible default because evaluation content is user-supplied.
suites_dir.title: Suites Dir

ConfigFile.title: ConfigFile

ErrorSummaryEntry.description:
One entry in :class:`RunReport.error_summary` (Requirement 11.3).

Emitted once per ``TestCaseResult`` whose ``status`` is ``error`` or
``timeout``. The entry repeats the identifying tuple from the
underlying result so the summary section can be rendered on its own
without cross-referencing ``RunReport.results`` (Property 22).

error_message.description: Human-readable error message from the underlying result.
error_message.title: Error Message

model.description: Ollama model tag the failed execution ran against.
model.title: Model

repetition.description: 1-indexed repetition number of the failed execution.
repetition.title: Repetition

suite.description: Evaluation_Suite name the failed test case belongs to.
suite.title: Suite

ErrorSummaryEntry.title: ErrorSummaryEntry

MetricAggregate.description:
Mean and population stddev for a single ``(model, metric)`` pair.

Keyed by metric name inside :class:`ModelAggregate.metric_aggregates`.
The aggregation itself is computed by :mod:`runner.aggregate` using
:func:`statistics.fmean` and :func:`statistics.pstdev` (Property 15).

``stddev`` is the *population* standard deviation, not the sample
variant. For a single-repetition Run (``count == 1``) this is the
well-defined value ``0``; sample stddev would be undefined. Sticking
to pstdev everywhere means the aggregator never has to special-case
``count == 1``.

count.description: Number of per-repetition samples that fed this aggregate. Must be >= 0.
count.title: Count

mean.description: Arithmetic mean of the per-repetition scores.
mean.title: Mean

metric.description: Metric name this aggregate corresponds to.
metric.title: Metric

stddev.description: Population standard deviation of the per-repetition scores. Must be >= 0.
stddev.title: Stddev

MetricAggregate.title: MetricAggregate

MetricResult.description:
Score produced by a single metric for a single Test_Case execution.

A ``MetricResult`` is always carried on a :class:`TestCaseResult`,
even when the metric itself raises during scoring (Requirement 7.5).
In the error path the runner sets ``error`` to the raised message and
``passed`` to ``False``; ``score`` is still required and the runner
records ``0.0`` by convention so the aggregation code can operate on
a uniform numeric field.

``details`` is a free-form mapping reserved for metric-specific
context (for example, the captured response for the
``response-capture`` metric, the matched regex group for
``regex-match``, or the JSON-Schema validation errors for
``json-schema-valid``). It intentionally has no internal schema here.

details.description: Metric-specific structured context. Free-form; validated by the metric implementation, not here.
details.title: Details

error.description: Populated when the metric raised during scoring (Requirement 7.5). ``passed`` is ``False`` in that case and the Run continues with the remaining metrics.
error.title: Error

name.description: Identifier of the metric that produced this result.
name.title: Name

passed.description: Pass/fail classification against the metric's threshold (Requirement 7.4).
passed.title: Passed

score.description: Numeric score in the metric-defined range (Requirement 7.3).
score.title: Score

threshold.description: Threshold used by the metric to classify ``score`` as passed. ``None`` for metrics that do not parametrise a threshold (e.g. boolean metrics).
threshold.title: Threshold

MetricResult.title: MetricResult

ModelAggregate.description:
Aggregate statistics for a single evaluated model.

One ``ModelAggregate`` is produced per entry in
``RunConfig.models``. Counters (``passed``/``failed``/``errored``/
``timed_out``) sum the per-execution ``TestCaseResult.status`` values
filtered to this model. Performance means average across every
``(test_case, repetition)`` for this model; ``mean_ttft_ms`` and
``mean_tokens_per_second`` are nullable because the underlying
per-execution fields are nullable (Requirement 6.5).

``metric_aggregates`` is keyed by metric name so the UI and
comparison report can look up a metric without scanning a list
(Property 19).

mean_tokens_per_second.description: Mean tokens-per-second, averaged across executions with a non-``None`` ``tokens_per_second``. ``None`` when no execution produced the derived value.
mean_tokens_per_second.title: Mean Tokens Per Second

mean_total_ms.description: Mean total response time in milliseconds, averaged across every execution for this model.
mean_total_ms.title: Mean Total Ms

mean_ttft_ms.description: Mean time-to-first-token in milliseconds, averaged across executions with a non-``None`` ``ttft_ms``. ``None`` when no execution reported TTFT.
mean_ttft_ms.title: Mean Ttft Ms

metric_aggregates.description: Per-metric mean/stddev aggregates keyed by metric name.
metric_aggregates.title: Metric Aggregates

model.description: Ollama model tag these aggregates describe.
model.title: Model

ModelAggregate.title: ModelAggregate

ModelInfo.description:
Identifying metadata for an evaluated Ollama model.

Populated from the Ollama_Server's ``/api/tags`` response at Run
start (Requirement 2.5). ``digest`` and ``parameter_size`` are
nullable because some Ollama_Server versions omit them and because
a Run submitted against an inventory that is still pulling a model
may run before the digest is observable.

digest.description: Content-addressed digest reported by the Ollama_Server.
digest.title: Digest

ModelInfo.title: ModelInfo

PerformanceMetrics.description:
Per-execution timing and token-count measurements.

``ttft_ms`` and the token-count fields are nullable because the
Ollama_Server does not always populate them (Requirement 6.5). A
missing value must not be coerced to ``0`` — ``0`` is a valid
measurement that means "zero tokens" or "no delay" and would be
indistinguishable from "unknown" if we silently defaulted to it.

``tokens_per_second`` is derived by the runner from ``response_tokens``
and ``total_ms`` (Requirement 6.4, Property 10). It is stored on the
model rather than recomputed on demand so that reports remain stable
even if the derivation rule changes in a future version.

prompt_tokens.description: Prompt token count reported by the Ollama_Server (Requirement 6.3). ``None`` when the server omitted it.
prompt_tokens.title: Prompt Tokens

response_tokens.description: Response token count reported by the Ollama_Server (Requirement 6.3). ``None`` when the server omitted it.
response_tokens.title: Response Tokens

tokens_per_second.description: Derived throughput: ``response_tokens / (total_ms / 1000)`` (Requirement 6.4). ``None`` when ``response_tokens`` is ``None`` or ``total_ms`` is 0.
tokens_per_second.title: Tokens Per Second

total_ms.description: Total response time from request dispatch to the final chunk, in milliseconds (Requirement 6.2).
total_ms.title: Total Ms

ttft_ms.description: Wall-clock time from request dispatch to the first streamed chunk, in milliseconds (Requirement 6.1). ``None`` when the server omitted the timing (Requirement 6.5).
ttft_ms.title: Ttft Ms

PerformanceMetrics.title: PerformanceMetrics

RunConfig.description:
Run-level configuration for a single Run.

``RunConfig`` is the body the Backend_API accepts for
``POST /api/runs`` and is also embedded in :class:`ConfigFile` as the
``run:`` section. A ``RunConfig`` is serialised verbatim into every
``RunReport`` so runs are reproducible from their report alone
(Requirement 8.4).

Validators enforce the numeric ranges specified in the Requirements
document and reject empty ``models`` / ``suites`` lists early, before
any Ollama_Server call is made.

concurrency.description: Maximum number of in-flight Ollama_Server requests during this Run. Must be >= 1 (Requirement 5.5).
concurrency.title: Concurrency

judge_model.description: Ollama model tag used by any configured ``llm-as-judge`` metric. ``None`` means no judge model is configured; metrics that require one will raise a validation error at Run start.
judge_model.title: Judge Model

models.description: Ollama model tags to evaluate in this Run (e.g. 'llama3:8b'). Must contain at least one model (Requirement 2.2).
models.title: Models

ollama_timeout_s.description: Per-request timeout in seconds applied to every Ollama_Server call made during this Run (Requirement 1.4). Must be > 0.
ollama_timeout_s.title: Ollama Timeout S

pull_missing_models.description: If true, instruct the Ollama_Server to pull any requested Model that is not already available (Requirement 2.4).
pull_missing_models.title: Pull Missing Models

repetitions.description: Number of times each Test_Case is executed per Model. Must be >= 1 (Requirement 5.2).
repetitions.title: Repetitions

retry_max_attempts.description: Number of *additional* retry attempts per Test_Case execution on retryable network errors (Requirement 11.1). Must be >= 0. A value of 0 disables retries entirely.
retry_max_attempts.title: Retry Max Attempts

suites.description: Names of Evaluation_Suites to run. Must contain at least one name (Requirement 3.1).
suites.title: Suites

tag_filter.description: Optional Test_Case tag filter (Requirement 3.6). When empty, every Test_Case in the selected suites is included. When non-empty, only Test_Cases whose ``tags`` intersect this list are selected.
tag_filter.title: Tag Filter

RunConfig.title: RunConfig

TestCaseResult.description:
Outcome of a single ``(model, test_case, repetition)`` execution.

``status`` is a four-value literal rather than a boolean because a
Run distinguishes four outcome classes in both the report and the
event stream (Property 9):

* ``pass`` — every configured metric classified the response as passing.
* ``fail`` — at least one metric classified the response as failing.
* ``error`` — the Ollama_Server call itself failed after all retries,
  or a metric raised in a way that the runner treats as a hard error.
* ``timeout`` — the Ollama_Server call exceeded ``ollama_timeout_s``.

``repetition`` is 1-indexed (the first repetition is ``1``, not ``0``)
so that the value can be displayed to users without re-indexing and
so that ``repetition <= RunConfig.repetitions`` is the natural bound.

error_message.description: Human-readable error message for ``error``/``timeout`` outcomes. ``None`` for ``pass``/``fail``.
error_message.title: Error Message

metrics.description: One ``MetricResult`` per configured metric on the test case, in the order declared on the ``TestCase``.
metrics.title: Metrics

model.description: Ollama model tag this execution ran against.
model.title: Model

performance.description: Timing and token-count measurements for this execution.

repetition.description: 1-indexed repetition number for this ``(model, test_case)`` pair. Must be >= 1.
repetition.title: Repetition

response.description: Raw model response text, when available. ``None`` for ``error``/``timeout`` outcomes that never produced output.
response.title: Response

suite.description: Name of the Evaluation_Suite the test case belongs to.
suite.title: Suite

TestCaseResult.title: TestCaseResult

description:
Top-level Run_Report persisted to disk and returned by the REST API.

A ``RunReport`` is the single source of truth for a completed Run
(Requirement 8.4): it embeds the full submitted :class:`ConfigFile`,
the identifying metadata for every evaluated model, every
per-execution result, the per-model aggregates, and an error
summary. Property 16 (Run_Report completeness) asserts this
invariant; Property 18 (round-trip) asserts that
``RunReport.model_validate_json(r.model_dump_json()) == r`` for
every valid ``r``.

``status`` is a five-value literal covering the full state machine:

* ``pending`` — the Run has been created but dispatch has not started.
* ``running`` — at least one execution is in flight or queued.
* ``completed`` — every planned execution has produced a result.
* ``aborted`` — the Run was stopped by a cancel request or signal
  before all planned executions completed (Requirement 11.4).
* ``failed`` — the Run could not start (e.g. missing models in
  preflight, Property 5) and no per-execution results exist.

The ``ended_at >= started_at`` invariant is enforced in a model
validator rather than field-by-field because it is a cross-field
constraint (Property 16).

backend_version.description: Version string of the Backend that produced this report.
backend_version.title: Backend Version

ended_at.description: UTC timestamp when the Run reached a terminal state. ``None`` while the Run is still ``pending`` or ``running``.
ended_at.title: Ended At

error_summary.description: One entry per result with ``status`` in ``{'error', 'timeout'}`` (Requirement 11.3).
error_summary.title: Error Summary

ollama_version.description: Ollama_Server version string at Run start. ``None`` when the Backend could not reach the server before failing.
ollama_version.title: Ollama Version

run_id.description: Unique identifier for this Run (Property 25). Must be non-empty.
run_id.title: Run Id

started_at.description: UTC timestamp when the Run started.
started_at.title: Started At

status.description: Terminal or in-flight state of the Run (see class docstring).
status.title: Status

title: RunReport
